v01.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 2
v01.description: Case 2 definition mixes and/or without parentheses: the "or" extends to the age/live-vaccine part, making the logic broader than intended.

v02.description: Due condition uses "...No measles... and age >= 12 months or No live vaccine..." without parentheses, changing conjunction grouping compared to the L2 row R2.

v03.description: Uses Encounter."MCV2 was administered" but the MCV2 rows in the L2 depend on MCV1 administered (and live-vaccine status).

v04.description: Due for MCV1 Row R2 swapped "No live vaccine..." to "Live vaccine..." (i.e., live-vaccine branch inverted), making due cases become not-due.

v05.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 1
v05.description: Uses Encounter."Client's age is less than 15 months" instead of "less than 12 months" for MCV1 Case 1.

v06.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 1
v06.description: Uses Encounter."Client's age is less than 9 months" instead of "less than 12 months" for MCV1 Case 1.

v07.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 2
v07.description: Case 2 missing the live-vaccine precondition (it includes only "No measles primary series doses were administered" and age >=12 months).

v09.description: Test validation expects Guidance string match but the scalar Guidance expression contains mismatch (one of the guidance branches uses != in a test), indicating guidance selection/aggregation diverges.

v10.description: In the test for Measles22.1, expects "Guidance" != '...come back for MCV1.' which is inconsistent with the canonical guidance; indicates mutated/incorrect guidance output.

v11.define: Client is due for MCV1 Guidance
v11.description: Client due for MCV1 Guidance is replaced with the 'Measles primary series is complete' guidance text.

v12.define: Client is due for MCV1 Guidance
v12.description: Client due for MCV1 Guidance text incorrectly references MCV2 in the guidance string.

v13.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 2
v13.description: Case 2 omits the live-vaccine precondition and/or uses wrong conjunction set compared to L2 Row R3.

v14.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 2
v14.description: Case 2 is present, but due/not-due pairing indicates the live-vaccine branch is inconsistent with L2 (due should correspond to R2, not R3).

v15.description: Due for MCV1 uses Row R2 pattern but the guidance/test alignment suggests the live-vaccine comparator inverted elsewhere; overall MCV1 due branch diverges from canonical.

v16.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Case 2
v16.description: Live-vaccine condition in Case 2 uses 'Live vaccine was administered in the last 4 weeks' but due/not-due aggregation indicates a comparator/branch inversion versus L2.

v17.description: Due for MCV1 appears to match R2, but downstream guidance aggregation differs (case precedence), making Guidance output inconsistent with L2.

v20.define: Client is not due for MCV1 Guidance
v20.description: In the guidance text for MCV1 Case 2, the ending sentence has MCV1 but an injected string mismatch is present compared with the canonical L2 guidance.
